reason_codes:
PIPELINE_ERROR:run_codex_exec failed after retries: run_codex_exec main stage failed: failed to launch codex main: runtime.run_command raised: context deadline exceeded: This error is likely due to exceeding 'timeout' — the total time a long running request (like process or directory watch) can be active. It can be modified by passing 'timeout' when making the request. Use '0' to disable the timeout.; launcher_cmd=bash -lc 'mkdir -p /home/user/workspace/outputs; touch /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_exec.log; rm -f /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_exec.pid /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_exec.rc; ( codex exec --skip-git-repo-check --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.1-codex-mini '"'"'You are running inside an E2B sandbox.
Hard constraints:
1) Work only in /home/user/workspace/repo
2) Read /home/user/workspace/inputs/task_spec.json and /home/user/workspace/inputs/claims_ir.json first
3) Run dependency_solver before execution (Codex-led but structured):
   - Detect dependency source in this order: pyproject.toml, requirements*.txt, environment.yml, setup.py
   - Decide install plan and command order
   - Record install events to codex_worklog.jsonl with type=install, command, result, and error summary
   - If pip is used, write install logs to /home/user/workspace/outputs/pip_install.log
   - Write dependency decisions and outcomes to /home/user/workspace/outputs/dependency_solver.json
   - Never silently retry unresolved install conflicts; write reason_codes and move on
4) Execute repo according to task_spec entrypoints/run_matrix
5) If one entrypoint cannot run due to dependency conflict, keep going for other entrypoints and record status+reason_codes in run_manifest
6) If all entrypoints are unrunnable due to dependencies, still write structured outputs and explicit dependency failure reason_codes
7) If execution fails, do minimal code fixes and retry (max 2 retries)
8) Write ALL required outputs to /home/user/workspace/outputs:
   - run_manifest.json
   - codex_worklog.jsonl
   - patches.diff
   - claim_alignment.json
9) claim_alignment MUST be concept-level only; do NOT include final numeric claim judgments.
10) The repo may not be a git repository. Do NOT stop because of git state.

JSON contracts:
- run_manifest.json: {"runs": [...], "reason_codes": [...]}
- claim_alignment.json: {"claims": [...], "reason_codes": [...]}

For each run_manifest item include:
run_id, command, params, cwd, exit_code, status, runtime_sec,
stdout_tail, stderr_tail, artifacts, metrics, reason_codes.

For each claim_alignment item include:
claim_id, required_metrics, source, evaluable(yes/no/partial), reason.

Produce deterministic, machine-readable outputs.'"'"'; rc=$?; printf '"'"'%s'"'"' "$rc" > /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_exec.rc ) 2>&1 | tee -a /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_main.log >> /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_exec.log & echo $! > /home/user/workspace/outputs/codex_exec.pid'. See artifacts/<run_id>/execution/codex_failure.json
